description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular wooden exterior and a large, flat glass screen dominating the front face. The front panel features two prominent round dials on the right side, likely for channel and volume control, and a metallic bezel framing the screen. Two thin metal antenna rods extend upward from the top surface, characteristic of older analog televisions. The sides and back are plain, finished in a wood-like texture, with no visible vents or ports. The overall design is utilitarian and retro, evoking mid-20th-century home electronics.
description: A retro box-shaped television with a wood-textured body, large glass screen, two control dials, and metal antennas on top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass screen, two round control dials on the right, and the metal antennas extending from the top.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain wooden side panel and the protruding control dials, with one antenna visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a featureless wooden back with both antennas visible, no ports or vents apparent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain wooden panel and a single antenna visible, no controls or screen in sight.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, metallic gray bezel with a simple rectangular shape and no visible branding
attribute_control_button_layout: two round dials on the front right side, arranged vertically
